title: RunManifest
description:
Top-level run manifest declaring one or more agent sessions.

Deserialized from TOML where sessions use the `[[sessions]]` array-of-tables
syntax. Each session references a spec and optionally overrides harness
configuration.

# Example TOML

```toml
[[sessions]]
spec = "auth-flow"

[[sessions]]
spec = "checkout"
name = "checkout-with-hooks"
```

sessions.description:
Ordered list of sessions to execute.

Maps to `[[sessions]]` in TOML. At least one session is expected,
but emptiness is a validation concern, not a deserialization concern.

HookContract.description: A lifecycle hook that runs an external command at a specific event.

command.description: Shell command to execute when the event fires.

event.description: The lifecycle event that triggers this hook.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the command to complete.

HookEvent.description:
Lifecycle event that the harness can hook into.

Hooks fire at specific points during agent execution:
- `PreTool`: before a tool invocation
- `PostTool`: after a tool invocation completes
- `Stop`: when the agent session is stopping

oneOf.description: Fires after a tool invocation completes.
Fires when the agent session is stopping.

ManifestSession.description:
A single session entry within a run manifest.

References a spec by name and optionally overrides harness configuration.
The pipeline merges these overrides with spec defaults and project config
to produce a complete [`HarnessProfile`](crate::HarnessProfile).

depends_on.description:
Sessions that must complete before this one can start.

Each entry is an effective session name (i.e. `name` if set, otherwise `spec`)
of another session in the same manifest. Used by the orchestrator to build an
execution DAG. Empty means no dependencies — the session can run immediately.

file_scope.description:
Glob patterns defining the file scope for this session.

When set, the harness enforces that the agent only modifies files
matching these patterns. Empty means no scope restriction.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks for this session. Empty by default.

name.description: Optional display name for this session. Defaults to the spec name if omitted.

prompt_layers.description: Prompt layers injected into this session's prompt assembly. Empty by default.

settings.description: Optional settings overrides applied to this session's harness profile.

shared_files.description:
Glob patterns for files shared with other sessions.

Files matching these patterns are expected to be touched by multiple
agents. The harness uses this to detect shared-file conflicts during
scope enforcement. Empty means no shared files declared.

spec.description: Name of the spec to run (required). Must match a spec file in the project.

PromptLayer.description:
A single prompt layer contributing to the assembled agent prompt.

Prompt layers are assembled in priority order (lowest `priority` value first)
to build the final system prompt for an agent session.

name.description: Human-readable name for identification and debugging.

priority.description: Ordering hint for assembly (lower values assemble first).

PromptLayerKind.description:
Category of a prompt layer, determining its priority tier in assembly.

Variants are ordered from highest built-in priority to custom:
- `System`: foundational agent instructions
- `Project`: project-level context and conventions
- `Spec`: spec-specific acceptance criteria and constraints
- `Custom`: user-defined layers injected at arbitrary priority

oneOf.description: User-defined layer injected at arbitrary priority.

SettingsOverride.description:
Settings overrides applied to an agent session.

All fields are optional or default-empty — only specified values override
the base configuration.

max_turns.description: Maximum number of agent turns before forced stop.
